title: Mini DBMS
summary: A from-scratch storage engine: slotted pages, a B+ tree index, and write-ahead logging.
highlights: Slotted page format that packs variable-length records without wasted space
B+ tree index with correct leaf vs internal node split rules
Write-ahead log so an interrupted write never corrupts a page
content:
## The problem

I wanted to understand how a database actually puts bytes on disk and finds them
again — not at the SQL layer, but underneath it, at the page and index level.

## The approach

I started at the storage layer: a fixed-size page is the unit of I/O, and a slotted
directory inside each page tracks where every record lives. On top of that sits a B+
tree for ordered lookups, and a write-ahead log for durability.

## Where it stands

The storage engine and B+ tree inserts work; the WAL and crash recovery are in
progress. The build logs live in the **Building a Mini DBMS** blog series.

title: RAFT + Key-Value Store
summary: A replicated key-value store built on a hand-rolled Raft consensus implementation.
highlights: Leader election and log replication implemented from the Raft paper
A linearizable key-value store layered on top of the replicated log
Fault-injection tests that kill and partition nodes mid-write
content:
## The problem

Distributed consensus is the part of systems work I understand least by reading and
most by building. Raft is designed to be understandable, which makes it the right
place to start.

## The approach

The plan is to implement Raft in stages — leader election first, then log
replication, then snapshotting — and only then layer a key-value store on top so the
consensus core stays testable in isolation.

## Where it stands

Planned. Design notes and the build series will land before the first commit.
